title: Contents
text: Contents “Written in Water” by Robert Ford “The Haunted Houses She Calls Her Own” by Gwendolyn Kiste “Mirror, Mirror” by Joshua Palmatier “Phoenix” by Mark Allan Gunnells “The Animal of Our Bodies” by Kristi DeMeester “O Adelín” by Michael Wehunt “Sharp Echoes” by Kelli Owen “Womb With a View” by Chad Lutzke “Midnight in the Southland” by Todd Keisling “Reverend Crow” by Joanna Koch “Rotten to the Core” by Richard Thomas “Back to One” by Jessica McHugh “Cabinet People” by Norman Prentiss “The Black Door” by Anthony J. Rapino Afterword by Kevin Lucia Preview Story: “A Mother is a Kind of God” by Eric LaRocca When I first read Robert Ford's short story “Georgie” (which I bought for Shroud Magazine's Halloween Issue so many years ago), I felt like my heart had been dug out of my chest with a dull ice cream scoop. It was not only a finely written Halloween ghost story, but it throbbed with an aching emotion I hadn't seen much of in horror fiction at that point. Since then, Robert's work has never failed to deliver an emotional gut-punch right to the center of my being. He has a knack for finding those secret pressure points we all have, and delivering just the right amount of pressure, driving us to the edge, which I'm sure he'll do with “Written in Water.” His most recent novel is Burner, one of the finest extreme-horror novels I've ever read.
